content.problemStatement:
There is a tech park, which consists of NNN rooms, numbered 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. The iii-th room has a cost of AiA_iAi​ associated with it. There are MMM bi-directional passages between these rooms.

Chef wants to create an office in this tech park, for which she will select exactly KKK out of these NNN rooms. These KKK rooms must be selected such that every selected room is reachable from every other selected room using some number of passages, and without passing through an unselected room.

The cost of creating the office is **not** the sum of the individual costs of the rooms, but rather the difference of the bitwise ORs and bitwise ANDs of the individual costs of the KKK rooms. 

Formally, let x1,x2,x3,...,xKx_1, x_2, x_3, ..., x_Kx1​,x2​,x3​,...,xK​ be the selected rooms. The cost of creating the office is (Ax1∣Ax2∣…∣AxK)(A_{x_1} | A_{x_2} | \ldots | A_{x_K})(Ax1​​∣Ax2​​∣…∣AxK​​) - (Ax1&Ax2&…&AxK)(A_{x_1} \& A_{x_2}  \& \ldots \& A_{x_K})(Ax1​​&Ax2​​&…&AxK​​), 

where '∣|∣' represents the bitwise OR operator, and '&\&&' represents the bitwise AND operator.

What is the minimum cost that must be incurred to create this office, if Chef selects the KKK rooms optimally? If it is not possible to select KKK rooms satisfying the given conditions, print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK — the number of rooms, passages and required number of rooms in the office respectively.
The next line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the cost associated with each of the rooms.
The next MMM lines describe the passages. The iii-th of these MMM lines contains two space-separated integers, uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting a passage between rooms uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible cost for that test case. Or output -1 if it is not possible to select KKK rooms.
constraints: ui≠viu_i \neq v_iui​=vi​ for each 1≤i≤M1 \leq i \leq M1≤i≤M.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: We can select nodes 222 and 333. A2A_2A2​ is 6 and A3A_3A3​ is 777. The cost is ((6∣7)−(6&7))=7−6=1((6 | 7) - (6 \& 7)) = 7 - 6 = 1((6∣7)−(6&7))=7−6=1.
Testcase 2: We can select nodes 111, 222 and 333. A1=1,A2=2A_1 = 1, A_2 = 2A1​=1,A2​=2, and A3=4A_3 = 4A3​=4. The cost is ((1∣2∣4)−(1&2&4))=7−0=7((1|2|4) - (1 \& 2 \& 4)) = 7 - 0 = 7((1∣2∣4)−(1&2&4))=7−0=7. It can be proven that a lower cost is not possible.
